scope_lock.source: Implement tenant-supplier-management (base_spec section 7) scoped to CRUD + read-only payable balance. Full-stack: harden the existing /tenant/suppliers backend to the customer-module contract, then wire the /nha-cung-cap screens off seed data (lib/suppliers, lib/debts) onto the real API. No debt ledger, no payment vouchers, no purchase-history rendering, no import/export, no credit-control editing.
in_scope: Tenant-scoped supplier list with search by name/code/phone and bounded pagination (pageSize <= 20)
Supplier detail read with contact fields, supplier type, tax code, and read-only payable balance
Supplier create and update with tenant isolation and validation (code and name both required)
Supplier soft delete preserving historical references (purchase, debt) via deletedAt + status INACTIVE
Read-only display of server-derived Outstanding Payable balance; supplier code uniqueness per tenant
Authenticated API client and real data wiring for /nha-cung-cap list, detail, form, and delete
Backend unit/controller/E2E and frontend client tests covering isolation, validation, duplicate-code conflict, and permission denial
out_of_scope: Supplier purchase/order history rendering (deferred to purchase spec)
Debt ledger management, payment vouchers (phieu chi), payables collection, or balance mutation from this module
Cooperation policy editing workflows: discount %, credit limit, payment term (credit control)
Import/export, bulk migration, deduplication, or merge
Advanced mode, RBAC changes, or new permission/feature namespaces

design_context.discovery_reason: Tenant-scoped CRUD mirroring the existing customers/suppliers module pattern. Supplier model, permissions (supplier:*), and the /tenant/suppliers controller+service already exist; no schema change or DB transaction is required. Work is contract-hardening plus frontend seed-to-API wiring.
design_context.validation_recommended_reason: Non-transactional CRUD slice with no schema/migration and no money mutation (balance stays read-only). Four task files under threshold; grounding + structural validation remain the quality floor.
